metadata.title: Neural Tangent Kernels for Axis-Aligned Tree Ensembles
organizations: National Institute of Informatics, The Graduate University for Advanced Studies, SOKENDAI

sections.title: Contribution
content: We formulate theNeural Tangent Kernel(NTK) induced by infinitely manyaxis-aligned differentiable tree ensembles.

sections.title: Differentiable Decision Tree
content: By employing a sigmoid-like function, we can relax the splitting into a continuous form, which allows the use of gradient descent.

figures.description: Differentiable decision tree architecture diagram showing input, inner node, leaves, and output formula

sections.title: Neural Tangent Kernel for Tree Ensembles
content: The NTK emerges in the context of functional gradient descent.
It does not change during training when considering infinitely wide neural networks.
Then, functional behavior becomes analytically tractable.
By handling thenumber of treeslike thewidth of neural networks, we can apply NTK theory to differentiable tree ensembles.
In this work, we extend the NTK concept foraxis-aligned trees.

figures.description: Functional gradient descent formula highlighting the Neural Tangent Kernel component
Legend for initial and trained model lines

sections.title: Closed-form NTK formula for Axis-Aligned Trees
content: Training behavior is nowanalytically tractableusing our formula.

figures.description: Detailed closed-form NTK formula for axis-aligned trees including contributions from internal nodes and leaves
Comparison of model output dynamics between analytical formula and finite ensembles (M=16, M=1024) for AAA and AAI

sections.title: Ensembles of Various Trees
content: The NTK induced by an ensemble of various tree architectures is equal to the sum of the NTKs induced by each architecture.

figures.description: NTK property formula for mixed tree ensembles

sections.title: Insight on Oblivious Trees
content: Any non-oblivious trees can be transformed into oblivious trees that induce exactly the same limiting NTK.

figures.description: Diagram showing the equivalence transformation between non-oblivious and oblivious tree structures

sections.title: Multiple Kernel Learning (MKL) as Architecture Search
content: MKL enables tree architecture search based on convex optimization.

figures.description: Bar chart showing MKL weights for different feature combinations in the tic-tac-toe dataset
Grid visualization of feature interactions for the tic-tac-toe endgame task
